raw: {"weights": {"R1.1": 0.3, "R1.2": 0.7, "R1.3": 0.7, "R1.4": 0.7, "R1.5": 0.0, "R1.6": 0.0, "R1.7": 0.3, "RX.1": 1.0, "RX.2": 1.0, "RX.3": 1.0, "RX.4": 1.0, "RX.5": 1.0}, "active_set": ["R1.2", "R1.3", "R1.4", "RX.1", "RX.2", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"], "rationale": {"R1.2": "The artifact contains a material internal contradiction: the abstract claims O(1/T) 'accelerated' convergence, but Theorem 1 as stated delivers O(1/sqrt(T)) — the classical non-accelerated rate. Additionally, the step-size formula invokes an L-Lipschitz gradient constant while the paper's core claim is applicability to non-smooth losses where no such Lipschitz gradient exists; this undermines the stated assumptions.", "R1.3": "The empirical case rests on a single dataset (CIFAR-10) at a single coverage level (0.7) with one metric ('30% fewer epochs'). The claim of being 'the first' and of broad applicability to a class of non-smooth losses is not substantiated by this narrow evidence base.", "R1.4": "Strong priority claims ('first mirror-descent procedure to deliver accelerated convergence on non-smooth selective losses') require verification against the selective-learning and mirror-descent literature; Cortes et al. and Chow are named but no direct comparison of rates or assumptions is given to establish the delta."}, "clarification_needed": true, "clarification_question": "Is your primary concern the internal mathematical correctness of the convergence claims (the O(1/T) vs O(1/sqrt(T)) discrepancy and the non-smooth/Lipschitz tension), or the adequacy of the empirical evidence (single dataset, single coverage) to support the stated contributions?"}

rationale.R1.2: The artifact contains a material internal contradiction: the abstract claims O(1/T) 'accelerated' convergence, but Theorem 1 as stated delivers O(1/sqrt(T)) — the classical non-accelerated rate. Additionally, the step-size formula invokes an L-Lipschitz gradient constant while the paper's core claim is applicability to non-smooth losses where no such Lipschitz gradient exists; this undermines the stated assumptions.
rationale.R1.3: The empirical case rests on a single dataset (CIFAR-10) at a single coverage level (0.7) with one metric ('30% fewer epochs'). The claim of being 'the first' and of broad applicability to a class of non-smooth losses is not substantiated by this narrow evidence base.
rationale.R1.4: Strong priority claims ('first mirror-descent procedure to deliver accelerated convergence on non-smooth selective losses') require verification against the selective-learning and mirror-descent literature; Cortes et al. and Chow are named but no direct comparison of rates or assumptions is given to establish the delta.

parsed.clarification_question: Is your primary concern the internal mathematical correctness of the convergence claims (the O(1/T) vs O(1/sqrt(T)) discrepancy and the non-smooth/Lipschitz tension), or the adequacy of the empirical evidence (single dataset, single coverage) to support the stated contributions?
